heroHeadline: Delegates Converge on Addis Ababa for Berlin Pre-Conference; AU Recommends Khartoum Office Reopening; 850+ RSF Foreign Mercenaries Disclosed
heroSubtitle: Sudanese delegates departed for Addis Ababa on April 9 for the April 10–12 preparatory session of the Third Berlin Conference on Sudan (April 15), while the National Forces Coordination confirmed its boycott, joining the National Forces Alliance in rejecting the process over exclusion concerns; AU Special Envoy Belaiche concluded a Khartoum assessment recommending resumption of AU operations in the capital — the first such visit since the AU evacuated in April 2023; military sources disclosed 850+ foreign mercenaries embedded with the RSF including Colombian 'Desert Wolves' drone specialists and Russian technical advisors, transited through Somalia and Libya, highlighting the conflict's deepening internationalization
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan
